topic: A student receives a much lower grade than expected
raw_output:
[story 1]

The red 'D-' slapped against the stark white of the paper, a harsh, undeniable verdict. Leo stared at the ink, his vision momentarily blurring as if the fluorescent lights of the library were buzzing too loudly. He traced the margin with a numb finger, the familiar knot tightening in his chest. Another setback. He let his head drop onto the desk, the cheap laminate cool against his forehead. The silence of the study room amplified the hollow echo of his disappointment. He didn't bother to look up at the textbook; the words swam, meaningless shapes mocking his effort. He just felt heavy, anchored by the sheer exhaustion of trying so hard and still falling short.

[story 2]

"So, that's it then?" Sarah mumbled, the sound barely audible above the distant hum of the campus ventilation. She folded the graded essay with deliberate, slow movements, the crisp edges of the paper seeming abrasive. Her shoulders slumped forward, the tension that had been coiled tight all afternoon finally unraveling into a slack, defeated posture. She picked up her pen, but her hand hovered over the page, refusing to commit to another line. A deep sigh escaped her, a sound that seemed to drain the last reserves of energy from her lungs. She just wanted the noise to stop, the pressure to lift, anything but the sharp sting of that low mark.

[story 3]

Mark leaned back in his chair, the springs groaning in protest beneath his weight. He rubbed his eyes with the heels of his palms, a slow, circular motion that did little to clear the fog behind his gaze. The grade was a blunt instrument, striking him squarely in the middle of his already frayed nerves. He picked up his water bottle, the cold plastic a sharp contrast to the dull ache spreading through his limbs. He didn't move to reread the feedback; the information felt too dense, too final. He just stared out the window at the grey afternoon sky, watching the clouds drift with an unhurried indifference, wishing for a quiet space where the constant need to perform simply dissolved.
